_**User**_

Review all DRAFT blog posts in /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog/ that start with __ (exclude __blog-template.md and __blank-blog-template.md) for JoRap voice issues per these rules:
- No em dashes (use hyphen, comma, parentheses)
- No AI slop (crucial, leverage, game-changer, let me know, When it comes to, etc.)
- First person, experiential, plain-spoken
- No engagement bait
- Recipe/how-to intros should sound human

For each file, return: filename, severity (ok/minor/major), specific issues with line quotes, and suggested fix summary. Skip posts that are only outlines with no body prose. Read the actual files.

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking the remaining draft posts — __worcestershire-sauce, __smart-plug, __rechargeable-batteries, and __password-managers — for voice issues.

__extension-cord contains full prose, so it is not an outline-only post. __wifi-router.md also appears to have real body text rather than being an outline. Now checking __password-managers for preachy tone and other voice issues.

All 24 drafts contain extensive em dashes. This systematic pattern violation will be treated as major severity. Minor issues include slight stiffness and isolated AI tells. Major issues include significant AI slop, preachy third-person voice, engagement bait, and hollow intros.

Severity will be calibrated by context. Posts with only a handful of em dashes and otherwise strong voice will be minor. Heavy em dash use paired with other voice violations will be major. The hard rule against em dashes applies even when they appear only in frontmatter.

Severity thresholds are now defined: ok means publishable voice with zero to two em dash issues; minor means good voice needing an em dash sweep; major means significant slop, framework voice, or thin prose. __wifi-router.md scored minor because its JoRap voice and experiential opener are solid, yet em dashes appear throughout the body.

__how-to-create-a-good-habit.md scored minor. Its personal voice is solid, yet em dashes clutter the body and the "optimize for outcome" quote feels frameworky.

__jobs-to-be-done-framework.md scored minor. It reads more like an educational framework than personal experience. The numbered list and "bottom line" sections feel template-like.

__there-is-no-perfect-solution.md scored ok. Its personal voice is strong and it carries only three em dashes.

__kewpie-mayo.md scored minor. Its pantry voice is strong, yet em dashes clutter the body.

__password-managers.md scored minor. The headline uses marketing language. The third-person objection section and the phrase "unlock your entire digital life" feel preachy.

__make-ai-articles-more-human.md scored major. Its quoted example prompt ironically contains the exact AI slop it warns against, such as "When it comes to" and "crucial." Heavy em dashes also clutter the draft.

__mi-box-s.md scored minor. Its personal review voice is solid, though em dashes appear throughout.

__rechargeable-batteries.md scored minor. The drawer story opener is effective, yet em dashes clutter the body.

__building-a-second-brain.md scored minor. Its opening rhetorical questions feel slightly generic.

__building-a-second-brain.md scored minor. The second-person shift and heavy em dashes need softening.

__personal-immune-booster-supplements.md scored minor. It feels like a notes export with thin lived detail and no personal story beyond line 20. Em dashes clutter the draft.

__stuart-mcgill-big-three-low-back-exercises.md is instructional and appropriate.

__stuart-mcgill-big-three-low-back-exercises.md scored minor. The "evidence-backed" phrase is slight inflation.

__process-over-outcomes.md scored minor. The opening quoted question feels essay-ish.

__worcestershire-sauce-lee-and-perrins.md scored minor. The pantry voice is strong.

__second-cellphone.md scored minor. The personal story opener is effective.

__remote-work-checklist.md scored minor. The word "elevates" is not slop.

__craft-prompt-framework.md scored minor. Its meta-prompt intentionally quotes slop; it needs framing commentary. __sustainable-online-publishing.md scored minor. Its opener uses third person instead of first person. __shopee-tips.md scored minor. __smart-plug.md scored minor.

__why-i-stopped-playing-marvel-snap.md scored minor. Its closing line is mild engagement advice, not bait. __canon-g3000.md scored minor. It lacks a personal ownership story despite good voice. __extension-cord.md scored minor. It is a full prose draft with a warm personal story.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Compiling the full review report.

Severity will be calibrated by voice issues rather than em dashes alone. Em dashes are a hard rule and will be quoted per file. A file with only em dashes gets minor severity. Other voice issues push it to minor or major.

make-ai-articles is major because its slop phrases violate the voice rules. Even the strongest drafts still contain em dashes, so none qualify as ok. Severity definitions are being re-checked to ensure strict application.

No draft qualifies as ok. Even the lightest em-dash counts—three to seven across several files—still push those posts to minor severity. __there-is-no-perfect-solution would be minor even with its strong voice, since it contains three em dashes.

Frontmatter fields like meta_title and description are included in the voice check.

__make-ai-articles-more-human is major because its example prompt itself contains the AI slop the skill warns against. It should either show a crossed-out bad prompt or explicitly warn readers not to copy it verbatim.

__craft-prompt-framework is minor. The word "comprehensive" sits inside a quoted meta-prompt, so it is intentional.

__sustainable-online-publishing is minor. Its opener uses third person instead of first person.

__building-a-second-brain is minor. It opens with a generic rhetorical phrase and then shifts to "we."

__personal-immune-booster-supplements is minor. It reads like a wiki export and needs a personal anecdote.

__password-managers is minor. It preaches to anyone and uses an unlock metaphor.

__jobs-to-be-done-framework is minor. It sounds like a textbook.

__canon-g3000 is minor. It lacks a personal ownership story.

None of the remaining drafts reach major severity.

__there-is-no-perfect-solution.md is minor. Two body em dashes and one in meta_title remain. The frontmatter still needs a fix. __wifi-router, __smart-plug, and __marvel-snap are the closest drafts to ok.

The report will open with a single batched note on em dashes, then list each file with severity, specific issues, and line quotes. Every draft contains body prose, so none are skipped. Em dashes will be counted per file and quoted where they dominate. Stronger posts will receive shorter em-dash notes. The quote from __how-to-create-a-good-habit is acceptable.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

## Batch note

Reviewed **24** `__*.md` drafts (excluded `__blog-template.md` and `__blank-blog-template.md`). **None skipped** — all have full body prose.

**Systemic issue:** Almost every draft uses em dashes (`—`) heavily in body copy and frontmatter. JoRap voice rule says use hyphen, comma, or parentheses instead. This is the most common fix across the batch.

**Strongest overall:** `__why-i-stopped-playing-marvel-snap.md`, `__wifi-router.md`, `__smart-plug.md`, `__extension-cord.md`, `__kewpie-mayo.md`

---

### `__there-is-no-perfect-solution.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** L3 meta_title, L18, L76 — e.g. `"That's the **nirvana fallacy** — comparing real options to an imaginary ideal"`
- **Otherwise:** Strong first-person, experiential opener, plain-spoken. No slop or bait.
- **Fix:** Light em-dash pass only (comma/parentheses). Lightest touch in the batch.

---

### `__why-i-stopped-playing-marvel-snap.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~10 in body — e.g. L14 `"snap on turn four — my brain knows the rhythm"`, L100 `"production value — all top-tier"`
- **Borderline engagement:** L102 `"If you're still playing and loving it: snap responsibly..."` — advice to reader, not "let me know" bait; fine if you want direct address.
- **Otherwise:** Best voice in batch. Specific, first-person, opinionated, no slop.
- **Fix:** Em-dash sweep. Optional: soften L102 if you want zero reader-facing sign-off.

---

### `__wifi-router.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** Throughout — e.g. L14 `"like printer ink — buy whatever's on sale"`, L42 `"**central and elevated** — hallway shelf"`
- **Otherwise:** Great experiential opener (video call drop, kid's homework). Personal checklist voice.
- **Fix:** Replace em dashes. "elevated" here is physical placement, not AI slop.

---

### `__smart-plug.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~13 — e.g. L26 `"behind a bookshelf — if reaching the switch is annoying"`, L56 `"On/off scheduling | Yes — the whole point"`
- **Otherwise:** Strong personal hook, honest limits section, no slop.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__extension-cord.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~10 — e.g. L14 `"duct tape of electricity — unglamorous"`, L34 `"something is wrong — undersized cord"`
- **Otherwise:** Personal warm-cord story, practical, first-person.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. (No longer outline-only.)

---

### `__kewpie-mayo.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** L14, L24, L38, L54 — e.g. `"I thought it was a gimmick — different bottle"`
- **"essential"** L68 `"Not essential for everyone"` — acceptable plain usage, not inflation.
- **Otherwise:** Human recipe/pantry intro, "how we use it" works.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__worcestershire-sauce-lee-and-perrins.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~13 — e.g. L16 `"Filipino kitchen — until you start adding"`, L32 `"before cooking — not enough to taste"`
- **Otherwise:** Good pantry voice, local context (adobo), human how-we-use sections.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__rechargeable-batteries.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~9 — e.g. L55 `"Smoke alarms | **No** — use long-life disposables"`
- **Otherwise:** Strong drawer opener, practical habits, first-person.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__mi-box-s.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~7 — e.g. L14 `"in name only — slow menus"`, L30 `"Chromecast built in — handy when"`
- **Otherwise:** Solid gear review, personal setup section.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__canon-g3000.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~8 — e.g. L14 `"Cartridge printers especially — cheap hardware"`, L83 `"**practical** — a refillable home printer"`
- **Thin experiential thread:** Opens with general printer rant, not "I owned a G3000 and..." No personal failure story.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Add one paragraph: why you bought it, what broke, what you'd buy again.

---

### `__second-cellphone.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** L4 description, L22, L35, L59, L67 — e.g. `"payments — modern life assumes"`
- **Mild marketing phrase:** L4 description `"peace of mind factor"` — vague/corporate.
- **Otherwise:** Strong swim-story opener, practical backup advice.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Rewrite description to something concrete ("SIM swap in ten minutes").

---

### `__shopee-tips.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~10 — e.g. L36 `"a lot of volume — Paranaque"`, L99 `"shop like I mean it — and close the app"`
- **Otherwise:** Strong PH-local voice, practical, first-person.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__remote-work-checklist.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~15 — e.g. L4 description, L37 `"**Power cord** — I have a dedicated bag cord"`
- **Otherwise:** Checklist format fits topic; personal pre-flight ritual.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__password-managers.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~11 — e.g. L14 `"avoiding hassle — you're **stockpiling"`
- **Slop-adjacent:** L14 `"unlocks your entire digital life"` — marketing unlock metaphor. L16 `"I recommend to anyone who'll listen"` — slightly preachy.
- **Meta_title:** L3 `"The One Security Upgrade Everyone Needs"` — absolute/clickbaity vs JoRap understated tone.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Soften L14–16 to first-person experience. Retitle meta_title to something like "Why I Use Bitwarden".

---

### `__how-to-create-a-good-habit.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~11 — e.g. L14 `"the default** — especially on tired weekdays"`, L82 `"after breakfast** — obvious cue"`
- **Otherwise:** Good personal failure section (L86–90). Framework content is expected for topic.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__process-over-outcomes.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** L3–4 frontmatter, L27, L49, L61 — e.g. `"graded on results — revenue, rankings"`
- **Otherwise:** Personal blogging/health/client applications. Quote opener is fine.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__jobs-to-be-done-framework.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~17 — heavy in numbered list — e.g. L52 `"**What is the main task or goal?** — the primary job"`
- **Less experiential:** Opens with teams observation, not personal JTBD failure. Reads more textbook than JoRap gear posts.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Add one real project where JTBD changed a decision.

---

### `__building-a-second-brain.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~20 — e.g. L16 `"**Building a Second Brain** — Tiago Forte's framing"`, L36 `"Save what resonates — quotes, links"`
- **Generic opener:** L14–15 `"How many good ideas have you had and forgotten?"` — hollow question pattern.
- **Voice drift:** L24 shifts to `"We read, listen..."` — less first-person than rest of site.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Rewrite opener from your own forgotten-idea moment. Change L24 to "I read...".

---

### `__second-brain-daily-workflow.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~15 — e.g. L22 `"**Middle-click a link** — open in a new tab"`, L68 `"**YouTube transcript** — get the words"`
- **Otherwise:** Personal habits, links to your other posts, good specifics.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__sustainable-online-publishing.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~15 — e.g. L22 `"TikTok — they're useful **distribution**"`
- **Not first-person opener:** L14 `"A front-end developer should be able to..."` — third-person/general, not "I publish on my own site because..."
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Rewrite L14 as "I wanted to showcase work on the web without begging an algorithm."

---

### `__stuart-mcgill-big-three-low-back-exercises.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~9 — e.g. L16 `"**Big Three** — curl-up, side plank"`, L35 `"slightly — **minimal movement**"`
- **Mild inflation:** L16 `"evidence-backed"` — slight significance puff; acceptable for health post.
- **Otherwise:** Honest disclaimer, "I sit for work" — good.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass.

---

### `__personal-immune-booster-supplements.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~13 — e.g. L30 `"as a cure — more **early-season support**"`, L87 `"**Sleep** — seven hours"`
- **Thin experiential:** Reads like wiki export / supplement catalog. L20 has brief personal context; no story of getting sick, what you actually take daily, what you'd skip.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Add one concrete routine paragraph (morning stack, what changed after you started).

---

### `__craft-prompt-framework.md` — **minor**
- **Em dashes:** ~16 — e.g. L50 `"CRAFT sections — Context, Role"`
- **Quoted slop in meta-prompt:** L39 `"comprehensive details"` inside code block — intentional example, but reads as slop without framing.
- **Hyperbolic title:** L16 `"The Ultimate Prompt To Write The Best Prompts"` — quoted from notes, still clickbaity.
- **Fix:** Em-dash pass. Add one line before the code block: "This is the skeleton I use; swap 'comprehensive' for specifics." Or rewrite the quoted prompt.

---

### `__make-ai-articles-more-human.md` — **major**
- **Em dashes:** ~14 throughout body.
- **AI slop in post body (quoted prompt):** L35 `"When it comes to writing content, two factors are crucial"` — exact phrases the JoRap voice skill bans, presented without clear "don't write like this" framing.
- **Irony risk:** Post teaches de-AIing but embeds the worst offender verbatim.
- **Otherwise:** Good workflow section (L53–55), honest "when I don't bother" section.
- **Fix:** Rewrite the L34–38 prompt to model good instructions (no "When it comes to," no "crucial"). Or keep as a **bad example** with explicit callout: "This is the viral slop prompt; I don't use it verbatim anymore." Em-dash pass on commentary.

---

## Severity summary

| Severity | Count | Files |
|----------|-------|-------|
| **ok** | 0 | — |
| **minor** | 23 | All except below |
| **major** | 1 | `__make-ai-articles-more-human.md` |

## Recommended batch fix order

1. **`__make-ai-articles-more-human.md`** — rewrite or reframe the example prompt (slop phrases).
2. **Global em-dash sweep** — all 24 files; start with frontmatter `meta_title`/`description`, then body.
3. **Thin-experience passes** — `__canon-g3000.md`, `__personal-immune-booster-supplements.md`, `__building-a-second-brain.md`, `__sustainable-online-publishing.md` (openers + personal thread).
4. **Tone tweaks** — `__password-managers.md` meta_title and preachy lines; `__second-cellphone.md` description.

No engagement bait ("let me know," "hope this helps," "what do you think") found in any draft. Recipe/pantry posts (`kewpie`, `worcestershire`) sound human in intros.
